description: Benchmark dataset for evaluating LLM capabilities in yeast synthetic biology pathway design. Each case is sourced from a 2025-2026 high-impact journal publication.

paper.title: Discover the maze-like network for glabridin biosynthesis

modules.description: Maze-like multi-route tailoring network. 13 theoretical routes identified, 6 functional pathways validated. Features a protection-deprotection mechanism via dynamic methylation-demethylation cycle.

additional_gene.name: StsTAL
additional_gene.function: Tyrosine ammonia-lyase, supplementary entry into phenylpropanoid pathway

engineering_strategies: Overexpression of tHMG1 (truncated HMG-CoA reductase) for mevalonate pathway flux
Overexpression of IDI1 (isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase) for DMAPP supply
Dynamic regulation of ERG20 for DMAPP/FPP balance
NADPH supply enhancement
Cyanamide-inducible DDI2 promoter for temporal control of GgDMT1
key_findings: 13 theoretical biosynthetic routes identified, 6 validated as functional
Ladder-like multi-route tailoring network discovered
Dynamic methylation-demethylation cycle acts as protection-deprotection mechanism
Multi-route redundancy confers higher robustness and yield than single-route design
183 licorice transcriptomes analyzed to identify 4 classes of scaffold-modifying enzymes

paper.title: Electron transfer engineering of artificially designed cell factory for complete biosynthesis of steroids

primary.name: Pregnenolone
applications: Precursor to all steroid hormones (progesterone, cortisol, testosterone, estradiol)

pathway.type: de novo biosynthesis from glucose via mevalonate pathway

modules.description: Strengthened endogenous MVA pathway for increased precursor supply

steps.note: Integrated into TY1 multiple copy sites

steps.enzyme_full_name: Mevalonate kinase, phosphomevalonate kinase, mevalonate diphosphate decarboxylase + IPP isomerase
steps.note: IDI1 integrated into TY1 multiple copy sites

modules.description: Squalene cyclization and sterol modifications via endogenous and heterologous enzymes

steps.enzyme_full_name: C-14 sterol reductase, C-4 methyl sterol oxidase, C-3 sterol dehydrogenase, 3-keto sterol reductase
C-8 sterol isomerase, C-5 sterol desaturase
steps.note: Integrated into TY3 multiple copy sites for enhanced flux
Dual copy inserted at ERG5/ERG6 knockout sites

gene_knockouts.reason: Prevent competing ergosterol synthesis (C-22 sterol desaturase)
Prevent competing ergosterol synthesis (sterol C-24 methyltransferase)

modules.description: Final reduction catalyzed by engineered DHCR7 with optimized electron transfer

mutations.electron_transfer_chain: F430Y + F434Y (shorten chain from 29.4 to 9.5 Å, 68% reduction)

modules.description: P450-mediated side-chain cleavage with engineered electron transfer

steps.enzyme_full_name: P450 sterol side-chain cleavage enzyme + Cytochrome P450 reductase (from Arabidopsis thaliana)

mutations.electron_transfer: GGGGS flexible linker connecting CYP87A3 to ATR (+37% pregnenolone)

engineering_strategies: Electron transfer engineering (ETE): shortening and stabilizing intramolecular electron transfer chains
DHCR7 protein engineering: 7 mutations across 3 functional domains (SRD, SBD, NADPH-binding)
CYP87A3 (P450scc) engineering: active site mutations + GGGGS linker fusion to reductase ATR
NADPH regeneration: ADR + ADX (Homo sapiens) for animal-derived electron transfer pathway
GDH (Bacillus subtilis glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase) for NADPH recycling
FPK + PTA (Bifidobacterium breve + Clostridium kluyveri) integrated into pentose phosphate pathway
NADPH/NADP+ ratio improved from 0.3 to 0.8
ERG5/ERG6 knockout to block competing ergosterol pathway
tHMG1 and IDI1 multi-copy integration at TY1 sites for MVA pathway flux
ERG2 and ERG3 multi-copy integration at TY3 sites for post-squalene flux

key_results.improvement_over_prior: ~11-fold improvement in pregnenolone titer vs. prior reports

key_findings: Electron transfer engineering (ETE) shortens electron transfer chains and stabilizes proton-coupled electron transfer
DHCR7 electron transfer chain shortened from 29.4 Å to 9.5 Å (68% reduction), yielding 5.93-fold cholesterol improvement
CYP87A3 deprotonation energy barrier reduced from 13.5 to 9.1 kcal/mol via A281D + T285E mutations
NADPH/NADP+ ratio engineering (0.3→0.8) sustains high-flux steroid biosynthesis
Complete de novo steroid biosynthesis from glucose achieved in S. cerevisiae with g/L-level titers

paper.title: Engineering metazoan fatty acid synthase to control chain length applied in yeast

target_product.name: Short- and medium-chain fatty acids (SMCFAs: C8, C10, C12)
applications: Palm oil alternative (sustainable replacement for C10/C12 lauric oils)

pathway.type: De novo fatty acid biosynthesis with engineered chain-length control

steps.enzyme_full_name: Pyruvate dehydrogenase complex / pyruvate decarboxylase pathway

modules.module_name: Engineered metazoan fatty acid synthase (mFAS) for chain-length control
modules.description: Heterologous metazoan FAS (type I megasynthase, ~270 kDa per chain) replaces or supplements native yeast FAS. The iterative FA elongation cycle (KS→KR→DH→ER→KS) is tuned by KS domain mutations to control premature chain release by the thioesterase (TE) domain.

steps.note: Condensation: chain elongation by 2 carbons per cycle
Cycle repeats: C4→C6→C8→C10→C12→C14→C16

steps.substrate: Acyl-ACP (chain length determined by KS variant)
steps.product: Free fatty acid (C8, C10, C12, or C14)
steps.note: Chain release timing controlled by KS-TE kinetic interplay

key_mutations.mechanism: Single amino acid exchange at position G113 in the KS active site tunnel modulates the steric gate that controls chain elongation vs. premature termination

engineering_strategies: Heterologous expression of metazoan FAS (mFAS) as a programmable type I megasynthase
Single amino acid substitution at KS domain G113 to redirect chain-length specificity
TE domain kinetics tuned to match KS elongation rate for targeted chain release
Yeast cell factory integration with native acetyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA supply
key_findings: Single amino acid exchanges at KS G113 redirect FA profiles: G113W→C8, G113F→C8/C10, G113M→C12/C14
Modular platform for programmable FA chain-length synthesis
674 mg/L C10/C12-FAs achieved with 67% purity
Demonstrates sustainable bioproduction route as palm oil alternative

paper.title: Engineering yeast peroxisome assembly enables the increased production of acetyl-CoA and its derived 5-deoxyflavonoids

pathway.type: De novo biosynthesis from glucose via phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathway

modules.description: PEX-mediated peroxisome engineering to boost cytosolic acetyl-CoA by up to 98%. Hybrid peroxisomes constructed by transplanting selected PEXs from Y. lipolytica into S. cerevisiae.

steps.note: Enhanced peroxisome number, size, and β-oxidation activity via PEX gene engineering
CHR is critical for 5-deoxy branch; without CHR, CHS produces naringenin chalcone instead

engineering_strategies: PEX (peroxin) gene engineering to modulate peroxisome assembly, import, and proliferation
Hybrid peroxisomes: transplanting Y. lipolytica PEXs into S. cerevisiae
Cytosolic acetyl-CoA supply increased by 98% via peroxisome remodeling
Malonyl-CoA pool enhancement for flavonoid biosynthesis
Phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathway gene expression optimization
key_findings: PEX-mediated peroxisome engineering as a general strategy for acetyl-CoA supply
Hybrid peroxisomes (Y. lipolytica PEXs in S. cerevisiae) significantly boost acetyl-CoA
1102.4 mg/L liquiritigenin, highest reported titer in yeast
Strategy extensible across yeast species and to other acetyl-CoA-derived products

paper.title: Metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae for de novo biosynthesis of hydroxytyrosol and salidroside

pathway.type: De novo biosynthesis from glucose via Ehrlich pathway and tailoring

modules.description: Via shikimate pathway and Ehrlich pathway

steps.enzyme_full_name: 4-Hydroxyphenylacetate 3-hydroxylase (from Pseudomonas aeruginosa) + Flavin reductase (from E. coli)

steps.note: Glucosylation at C-8 hydroxyl of tyrosol
SUC2 deleted to redirect sucrose metabolism

engineering_strategies: Tyrosol chassis strain (ZYT1) optimization: 571.8 mg/L tyrosol
Heterologous PaHpaB + EcHpaC for tyrosol hydroxylation to hydroxytyrosol
RrU8GT33 glycosyltransferase for tyrosol glucosylation to salidroside
UDP-glucose supply enhancement via truncated sucrose synthase (tGuSUS1)
SUC2 deletion to redirect carbon flux from sucrose hydrolysis to UDP-glucose synthesis
Auxotrophic repair for strain robustness

key_results.hydroxytyrosol_titer: 304.4 mg/L (shake flask) / 677.6 mg/L (15-L bioreactor, strain ZYHT1)
key_results.salidroside_titer: 1021.0 mg/L (shake flask) / 18.9 g/L (fed-batch fermentation, strain ZYSAL9+3)

key_findings: 18.9 g/L salidroside in fed-batch, highest reported microbial titer
UDP-glucose supply engineering via sucrose synthase is key to high glycoside titers
Modular platform: same tyrosol chassis enables both hydroxytyrosol and salidroside branches
SUC2 deletion coupled with tGuSUS1 efficiently redirects sucrose carbon to UDP-glucose

paper.title: Enhanced heme production in industrial Saccharomyces cerevisiae through metabolic engineering

target_product.name: Heme (iron protoporphyrin IX)
applications: Plant-based meat coloring and flavor (heme gives meat its red color and taste)

pathway.type: Enhancement of endogenous heme biosynthetic pathway

modules.description: Endogenous pathway from succinyl-CoA + glycine to heme, with rate-limiting steps derepressed

steps.note: First committed step
Rate-limiting enzyme #1
Rate-limiting enzyme #2
Rate-limiting enzyme #3
Rate-limiting enzyme #4

engineering_strategies: Overexpression of 4 rate-limiting enzymes: HEM2, HEM3, HEM12, HEM13
Knockout of HMX1 (heme oxygenase) to block heme degradation
CRISPR/Cas9-based genome editing
Industrial strain background (KCCM 12638) with naturally high heme levels
Glucose-limited fed-batch fermentation for maximal heme accumulation
key_findings: HEM2, HEM3, HEM12, HEM13 identified as rate-limiting steps in heme pathway
HMX1 knockout prevents heme degradation to biliverdin
Industrial whisky starter strain (KCCM 12638) used as robust chassis
67 mg/L heme in fed-batch, relevant for plant-based meat applications

paper.title: De Novo Biosynthesis of Biochanin A in Saccharomyces cerevisiae via Integrated Metabolic and Organelle Engineering
paper.journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

pathway.type: De novo biosynthesis from glucose via phenylpropanoid → flavonoid → isoflavone pathway

steps.note: Key CYP450 step; copy number optimization critical
Regioselective methylation at 4'-OH position

engineering_strategies: PlIFS (isoflavone synthase) copy number optimization → 16.10 mg/L genistein platform
Screening of PlOMT9 as optimal 4'-O-methyltransferase → 14.29 mg/L biochanin A
Vitreoscilla hemoglobin (VHb) expression to enhance CYP450 activity → 19.48 mg/L
Endoplasmic reticulum (ER) membrane expansion for improved membrane-bound CYP450 expression → 39.89 mg/L
Protein-folding chaperone coexpression for functional CYP450 folding
Heme supply improvement to support CYP450 activity → 49.86 mg/L
key_findings: First de novo biochanin A production in S. cerevisiae
ER membrane expansion + chaperone coexpression doubles CYP450 activity
Heme supply is limiting factor for isoflavone synthase (CYP450) activity
Integrated metabolic + organelle engineering strategy for membrane-bound enzyme optimization

paper.title: Metabolic Engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae for the Efficient Production of Human Milk Oligosaccharide, Lacto-N-neotetraose (LNnT)

pathway.type: De novo biosynthesis from lactose + glucose via nucleotide sugar pathway

steps.enzyme_full_name: Glucosamine-6-P acetyltransferase, N-acetylglucosamine phosphomutase, UDP-GlcNAc pyrophosphorylase

engineering_strategies: Coexpression of LgtA, LgtB, Lac12, and Gal10 (strain LN1 → 1.54 g/L)
Short peptide tag assembly of LgtA and LgtB for proximity channeling → 2.42 g/L
UDP-GlcNAc and UDP-galactose supply pathway optimization
Fed-batch fermentation optimization → 6.25 g/L
key_findings: 6.25 g/L LNnT, highest reported in S. cerevisiae
Peptide tag-based enzyme assembly of LgtA-LgtB improves substrate channeling
GRAS-status yeast chassis suitable for food-grade HMO production
Scalable approach for infant formula ingredient manufacturing

paper.title: Engineering Saccharomyces cerevisiae for De Novo Biosynthesis of 3'-Hydroxygenistein
paper.journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

target_product.name: 3'-Hydroxygenistein
applications: Enhanced estrogenic and antioxidant activity compared to genistein

pathway.type: De novo biosynthesis from glucose via isoflavone pathway + regioselective hydroxylation

steps.note: Multi-copy integration for enhanced conversion

engineering_strategies: PlIFS copy number amplification for genistein platform (44.55 mg/L genistein)
Screening and multi-copy integration of isoflavone 3'-hydroxylase (I3'H)
Heme cofactor supply enhancement for dual CYP450 (IFS + I3'H) activity
CPR (cytochrome P450 reductase) pairing optimization
key_findings: First de novo biosynthesis of 3'-hydroxygenistein in S. cerevisiae
Dual CYP450 cascade (IFS → I3'H) requires coordinated heme and CPR supply
Genistein platform strain (44.55 mg/L) serves as foundation for hydroxylated derivatives

paper.title: Adaptively Evolved and Multiplexed Engineered Saccharomyces cerevisiae for Neutralizer-Free Production of l-Lactic Acid
paper.journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

host_organism.evolved_strain: TMG2 (acid-tolerant mutant via ALE, tolerates pH 2.60)

pathway.type: Heterologous L-lactate dehydrogenase + endogenous pyruvate supply

modules.description: Reduction of ethanol and glycerol byproduct formation

steps.note: Overexpression relieves intracellular lactic acid toxicity

engineering_strategies: Adaptive laboratory evolution (ALE) for acid tolerance → TMG2 mutant (pH 2.60 tolerant)
Push-pull-restrain strategy: enhance glycolysis (push), overexpress LDH (pull), suppress PDC (restrain)
PfFNT transporter overexpression for efficient lactic acid export
NADH supply and acetyl-CoA metabolism optimization
Neutralizer-free fermentation process (no CaCO3 needed)
key_findings: 76.2 g/L L-lactic acid without any neutralizer, at pH 2.90
2.1 g/(L·h) productivity, highest ever reported for neutralizer-free yeast fermentation
ALE-derived TMG2 tolerates pH 2.60, enabling cost-effective downstream processing
PfFNT transporter from Plasmodium falciparum is effective lactate exporter in yeast
Push-pull-restrain strategy is a generalizable framework for organic acid production
